channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Red light cameras coming to Las Vegas? Lawmaker wants exception that would allow them
item.description: Traffic cameras to catch drivers in the act of running red lights would be allowed under a bill introduced Tuesday at the Nevada Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: EPA head says he'll roll back dozens of environmental regulations, including rules on climate change
item.description: The head of the Environmental Protection Agency has announced a series of actions to roll back landmark environmental regulations, including rules on pollution from coal-fired power plants, climate change and electric vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge dismisses lawsuit claiming tribal casinos in Minnesota are running illegal games
item.description: A federal district court judge has dismissed a lawsuit brought by one of Minnesota’s horse racing tracks against executives who oversee five tribal casinos in the state.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Olson, MPR News

item.title: In a blow to Democrats, Sen. Jeanne Shaheen will not seek reelection in New Hampshire
item.description: Democrats in Washington are trying to sound optimistic following Democratic Sen. Jeanne Shaheen's announcement she won't seek reelection in New Hampshire in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Steve Peoples, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump campaigned as a protector of free speech. Critics say his actions as president threaten it
item.description: President Donald Trump campaigned as a champion of free speech, but First Amendment advocates say they’ve never seen freedom of speech under attack the way it has been in his second term.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi And Christine Fernando, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Affordable housing threatened as Trump halts $1 billion slated for extending life of aging buildings
item.description: The Associated Press has obtained a document showing that the Trump administration is halting a $1 billion program that helps preserve affordable housing.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Digital license plate bill could include a way for Nevadans to pay registration in installments
item.description: GPS features, no stickers and possibly a way to pay your vehicle registration in installments. Those were some of the reasons that digital license plates might get support at the Nevada Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US judge temporarily halts Trump plan to cut hundreds of millions of dollars for teacher training
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge in Boston on Tuesday blocked the Trump administration's plan to cut hundreds of millions of dollars for teacher training, finding that cuts are already affecting training programs aimed at addressing a nationwide teacher shortage. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top House Democrats press Senate colleagues to sink GOP spending bill
item.description: The top House Democrats are pressing their fellow party members in the Senate to kill the Republicans’ spending bill when it reaches the upper chamber, saying the proposal will hurt Americans around the country. “I wouldn’t expect senators to vote for this,” said Rep. Pete Aguilar (Calif.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Law firm sues over Trump executive order that seeks to suspend security clearances
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A law firm targeted by President Donald Trump over its legal services during the 2016 presidential campaign sued the federal government Tuesday over an executive order that seeks to strip its attorneys of security clearances.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Department of Education lays off nearly half of workforce
item.description: The Department of Education announced Tuesday it was firing nearly half of its workforce, the latest Trump administration move to shrink the federal government that could face swift legal challenge.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans ask party to keep clean energy credits
item.description: The Republicans who signed the letter have not ruled out voting against a continuing resolution to fund the government.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Mark Carney, elected next Canadian prime minister
item.description: Mark Carney, a former central banker, won his Liberal Party's election in a landslide on Sunday to replace Justin Trudeau as prime minister of Canada. He will be sworn into office in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada Democrats urge Lombardo to hire fired federal workers
item.description: Democratic leaders in the Nevada Assembly are urging Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo to hire federal workers laid off as part of recent firings from President Donald Trump and Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News
